hook: Mixed signals are often less mysterious than they feel when you stop reading them only through hope.
body: Ambiguous behavior usually contains real information, even if that information is simply inconsistency or low capacity.

hook: Men often treat inconsistency like a code to crack instead of a pattern to evaluate.
body: You do not need a villain to conclude that the signal is not strong enough for healthy investment.

body: Warm texts followed by repeated cancellations may not mean hidden depth; they may simply mean unstable follow-through.

body: Judge mixed signals by the recurring pattern, not by the most flattering explanation available.
